title: TPMS Sensor Programming (Blank/Aftermarket Sensors)
description: Programs a blank or universal aftermarket TPMS sensor to match the OEM protocol before installation. Required when using universal sensors (Schrader EZ-sensor, Autel MX-sensor, etc.) instead of OEM sensors.
physical_solution: Use TPMS programming tool to clone OEM protocol onto blank sensor. Install programmed sensor in tire. Perform relearn procedure (see sensor_registration.json or tpms_service_positions.json).

uds_services: NOTE: Sensor programming requires dedicated TPMS tool (Autel TS508/TS601, ATEQ VT56, Bartec TECH400) — NOT ELM327. Post-installation relearn uses $31 RoutineControl via ELM327.
technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Limited — sensor programming cannot be done with ELM327. Requires TPMS programming tool with RF capability (315MHz or 433MHz). Post-programming relearn procedure accessible via ELM327 $31.
preconditions: Correct blank/universal sensor selected for vehicle application
Sensor valve stem type matched to wheel (rubber vs aluminum)

procedure_steps.description: Identify vehicle TPMS protocol and frequency. Use chart below (OEM notes). Select correct blank sensor for the protocol.

On TPMS programming tool: select OEM make/model/year. Select 'Program Sensor' or 'Clone'. Place blank sensor near tool antenna. Tool transmits protocol data to sensor via LF (low frequency) activation.
Verify programming: tool reads back programmed sensor to confirm ID and protocol match. Some tools assign random unique ID; others copy a specified ID.

Install programmed sensor in tire valve hole BEFORE mounting tire on rim. Torque sensor nut to spec: rubber-stem type = finger tight + 1/4 turn; aluminum clamp-style = 3.5-4.5 ft-lbs (do not overtorque — sensor body cracks).
Mount and inflate tire to vehicle spec. Confirm sensor is not damaged during mounting (stay clear of sensor with tire machine).
After installation: perform TPMS relearn procedure to register new sensor ID with TPMS ECU. See sensor_registration.json for relearn steps.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: Frequency: 315 MHz (North America) / 433 MHz (Europe/Asia). Protocol: Toyota-specific Calsonic/TDK-style. Sensor pressure range: 0-87 psi. Most Toyota sensors activate at cold 33 psi threshold. Popular compatible: Schrader 33500, Autel MX-sensor programmed to Toyota protocol.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: Frequency: 315 MHz (North America). Protocol: Honda-Nidec style. Threshold: typically 28 psi low warning. Compatible: most universal sensors support Honda 315MHz protocol. Acura RDX/MDX: same as Honda.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: Frequency: 315 MHz (North America pre-2014) / 433 MHz (some 2014+ and global). Protocol: Nissan-specific. Check model year and market carefully. Compatible: Schrader, Autel support both Nissan protocols.
oem_notes.Ford: Frequency: 315 MHz (North America). Protocol: Beru/Schrader Ford-specific. Some 2011-2013 F-150: 433 MHz (early adoption). Check model year. Compatible: Motorcraft OEM sensors or Schrader 20001 for most Ford applications.
oem_notes.GM: Frequency: 315 MHz (North America). Protocol: Delphi/Schrader GM-specific. Pressure threshold: typically 25% below recommended. Compatible: ACDelco 13586335 equivalent or Autel MX-sensor programmed to GM protocol.
oem_notes.BMW: Frequency: 433 MHz (worldwide). Protocol: BMW-specific RDC (Reifendruck Control). Sensor includes temperature monitoring. BMW RDC sensors store wheel position (FL/FR/RL/RR). Compatible: Schrader 5600 BMW, Continental A2C9606130080.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: Frequency: 433 MHz (worldwide). Protocol: VW/Audi-specific. MQB platform (2013+): updated protocol vs PQ35. Must match platform generation. Compatible: VDO/Continental S180052016Z for most MQB. Autel MX-sensor with VW-specific programming.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Frequency: 433 MHz (North America and worldwide). Protocol: Chrysler-specific. Note: switched from 315MHz to 433MHz around 2012-2013. Verify model year frequency. Compatible: Schrader 29020, Autel MX-sensor.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Frequency: 433 MHz (worldwide). Protocol: Hyundai/Kia-specific. Threshold: 25 psi typical. Genesis: same protocol as standard Hyundai on most models. Compatible: Autel MX-sensor, Schrader with Hyundai/Kia programming.
